decision_points: IF incident recurrence rate > 0.05 THEN execute ModelRetrainingTrigger
IF corrective action cycle time > 14 days THEN escalate to AIMSImprovement
rules: Every NonconformanceReport must be logged with timestamp and source within 24 hours
All CorrectiveAction records require linked KPI metrics for closure
ModelRetrainingTrigger must reference at least one BiasIncident or SafetyEvent
exceptions: SafetyEvent with critical severity bypasses standard cycle time and requires immediate CorrectiveAction within 4 hours
AuditFinding from external regulator overrides internal PerformanceGap priority
data_requirements: recurrence_rate: float calculated from historical CorrectiveAction logs
cycle_time_days: integer from CorrectiveAction start/end timestamps
success_criteria: AI corrective action cycle time <= KPI target
model incident recurrence rate decreased by >= 20% quarter-over-quarter
AIMSImprovement implemented and verified in next audit
failure_modes: Recurring incidents not detected due to missing linkage between NonconformanceReport and ModelRetrainingTrigger
CorrectiveAction delayed beyond KPI causing compliance flag violation

agent_spec.decision_logic: On trigger (NonconformanceReport/BiasIncident/SafetyEvent/AuditFinding/PerformanceGap) evaluate rules and decision_points: log within 24h, check recurrence_rate > 0.05 or cycle_time > 14 days, apply exceptions for critical SafetyEvent (4h action) or external AuditFinding, then execute linked CorrectiveAction or AIMSImprovement
escalation_rules: SafetyEvent critical severity not actioned in 4 hours

sop:
# SOP — AI Improvement — Nonconformity and Continual Improvement
**Process ID:** ISO42001-10
**Framework:** ISO 42001:2023 | **Domain:** ISO 42001
**Generated:** 2026-06-10

## Purpose
Management of AI-specific nonconformities including model failures, bias incidents, safety events and continual improvement of the AI management system

## Triggers
- New NonconformanceReport received
- BiasIncident logged
- SafetyEvent detected
- AuditFinding published
- PerformanceGap threshold exceeded

## Inputs Required
- AI nonconformance reports
- bias incidents
- safety events
- audit findings
- performance gaps

## Process Steps
1. IF incident recurrence rate > 0.05 THEN execute ModelRetrainingTrigger
2. IF corrective action cycle time > 14 days THEN escalate to AIMSImprovement

## Expected Outputs
- AI corrective actions
- model retraining triggers
- AIMS improvements
- lessons learned
- updated AI controls

## Business Rules
- Every NonconformanceReport must be logged with timestamp and source within 24 hours
- All CorrectiveAction records require linked KPI metrics for closure
- ModelRetrainingTrigger must reference at least one BiasIncident or SafetyEvent

## Exception Handling
- SafetyEvent with critical severity bypasses standard cycle time and requires immediate CorrectiveAction within 4 hours
- AuditFinding from external regulator overrides internal PerformanceGap priority

## Success Criteria
- AI corrective action cycle time <= KPI target
- model incident recurrence rate decreased by >= 20% quarter-over-quarter
- AIMSImprovement implemented and verified in next audit

## Compliance Requirements
- ISO 42001:2023 Clause 10
- EU AI Act post-market monitoring
- NIST AI RMF continual improvement
test_cases: Test 1: description of happy path test
deployment_notes: Agent ready for deployment. Compliance: ISO 42001:2023 Clause 10, EU AI Act post-market monitoring, NIST AI RMF continual improvement
